title: Health Officials Respond to Meningitis Outbreak in Berkshire
subtitle: Schools and colleges take action after a student dies from a rare infection.

summary.en: Health officials in the UK are managing a meningitis outbreak in Berkshire and Oxfordshire. One student has died, and two others are receiving medical treatment. The UK Health Security Agency is providing antibiotics to close contacts of the sick students. Experts state that the risk to the general public remains low because the disease does not spread easily. Officials are reminding young people to check their vaccination status as a safety measure.
summary.zh: 英國衛生官員正處理伯克郡與牛津郡的腦膜炎疫情。目前已有一名學生死亡，另有兩名學生正在接受治療。英國衛生安全局正向與患病學生密切接觸者提供抗生素。專家表示，由於該疾病不易傳播，對一般大眾的風險仍然很低。官員也提醒年輕人檢查疫苗接種狀態，以作為安全防護措施。

paragraphs.en: Health officials in England are working to manage an outbreak of meningitis in the Berkshire area. The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) confirmed that one student has died from the infection, while two other students are currently receiving medical treatment. The situation has caused concern among parents and staff at several schools, but experts are working to prevent the illness from spreading further.
paragraphs.zh: 英國衛生官員正致力於控制伯克郡（Berkshire）地區的腦膜炎疫情。英國衛生安全局（UKHSA）證實，已有一名學生因感染死亡，另有兩名學生目前正在接受治療。此情況已引起多所學校家長與教職員的擔憂，但專家正積極採取行動，防止疾病進一步擴散。

paragraphs.en: The student who passed away attended Henley College in Oxfordshire. Meanwhile, the two other patients are students at Reading Blue Coat school and Highdown secondary school and sixth form centre in Berkshire. According to The Guardian Health, the UKHSA is working closely with these schools to provide support and information to the community. As a precaution, health workers have offered antibiotics—medicine used to kill bacteria—to people who have been in close contact with the sick students.
paragraphs.zh: 不幸過世的學生就讀於牛津郡（Oxfordshire）的亨利學院（Henley College）。與此同時，另外兩名患者分別就讀於伯克郡的雷丁藍袍學校（Reading Blue Coat school）以及海當中學暨預科中心（Highdown secondary school and sixth form centre）。根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，英國衛生安全局正與這些學校密切合作，為社區提供支援與資訊。作為預防措施，醫護人員已向曾與患病學生密切接觸的人員提供抗生素，這是一種用於殺滅細菌的藥物。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Rachel Mearkle, a consultant in health protection, offered her condolences to the family and friends of the student who died. She explained that while many people are naturally worried about the possibility of more cases, the public should know that meningococcal meningitis does not spread easily. It requires very close contact between individuals to pass from one person to another. Because of this, the risk to the wider public is considered low. Dr. Mearkle noted that large outbreaks of this disease are rare, though a recent, separate outbreak in Kent did lead to two deaths and several hospitalizations earlier this year.
paragraphs.zh: 健康保護顧問瑞秋·米爾克（Rachel Mearkle）醫師向過世學生的親友表達了哀悼。她解釋說，雖然許多人自然會擔心出現更多病例，但大眾應了解腦膜炎雙球菌腦膜炎並不容易傳播。它需要人與人之間非常密切的接觸才會傳染。因此，對廣大公眾而言，風險被認為很低。米爾克醫師指出，此類疾病的大規模爆發相當罕見，儘管今年稍早肯特郡（Kent）曾發生一起獨立的疫情，導致兩人死亡及多人住院。

paragraphs.en: Experts have noted that this current outbreak in Berkshire involves a specific strain of meningitis B. Andrew Preston, a professor at the University of Bath, explained that the cases appear to be contained within a small group of people who know each other. This makes it easier for health officials to find those at risk and provide them with necessary medicine or vaccinations. Professor Preston added that there is currently no sign that the infection is spreading to the general community.
paragraphs.zh: 專家指出，目前伯克郡的這波疫情涉及特定菌株的 B 型腦膜炎。巴斯大學（University of Bath）教授安德魯·普雷斯頓（Andrew Preston）解釋說，這些病例似乎侷限於一個彼此相識的小群體內。這使得衛生官員更容易找出高風險群體，並提供必要的藥物或疫苗。普雷斯頓教授補充說，目前沒有跡象顯示感染正擴散至一般社區。

paragraphs.en: Meningitis is an infection that can be very serious and needs quick medical attention. The UKHSA reports that between 300 and 400 cases of meningococcal disease are diagnosed in England every year. It most commonly affects babies, young children, teenagers, and young adults. Common signs and symptoms include a high fever, a severe headache, rapid breathing, feeling very sleepy or drowsy, shivering, vomiting, and having cold hands and feet. In some cases, the infection can lead to sepsis, which may cause a rash that does not fade when a glass is pressed against it.
paragraphs.zh: 腦膜炎是一種可能非常嚴重且需要迅速就醫的感染。英國衛生安全局報告稱，英格蘭每年診斷出 300 到 400 例腦膜炎雙球菌疾病。它最常影響嬰兒、幼童、青少年及年輕成人。常見徵兆與症狀包括高燒、劇烈頭痛、呼吸急促、感到非常嗜睡或昏沉、發冷、嘔吐，以及手腳冰冷。在某些情況下，感染可能導致敗血症，這可能會引發皮疹，且當用玻璃杯壓住皮疹時，皮疹不會褪色。

paragraphs.en: Health officials are using this time to remind young people about the importance of vaccinations. The MenB vaccine has been part of the regular childhood vaccination program in the UK since 2015. Additionally, the NHS offers the MenACWY vaccine to students in school years 9 and 10, and it remains free for young people until they reach the age of 25. While these vaccines do not protect against every single strain of the disease, they are a vital tool for staying healthy. The UKHSA emphasizes that it is important for everyone to recognize the symptoms of meningitis, regardless of whether they have been vaccinated or not.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生官員正藉此機會提醒年輕人疫苗接種的重要性。B 型腦膜炎疫苗自 2015 年起已納入英國的常規兒童疫苗接種計畫。此外，英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS）也為 9 年級與 10 年級的學生提供 ACWY 型腦膜炎疫苗，且 25 歲以下的年輕人皆可免費接種。雖然這些疫苗無法預防該疾病的所有菌株，但它們是保持健康的重要工具。英國衛生安全局強調，無論是否接種過疫苗，每個人了解腦膜炎的症狀都非常重要。

paragraphs.en: Both Henley College and the other affected schools have stated that they are following the guidance provided by the UKHSA. They are focusing on supporting their students and staff during this difficult time. While the situation remains serious, the quick response from health authorities and the schools is intended to keep the community safe. Officials continue to monitor the situation closely and have promised to provide updates if necessary. For now, the main advice from health experts is to stay calm, watch for symptoms, and seek medical help immediately if there is any concern about a person’s health.
paragraphs.zh: 亨利學院與其他受影響的學校皆表示，他們正遵循英國衛生安全局提供的指導方針。他們正專注於在這段困難時期為學生與教職員提供支援。儘管情況依然嚴峻，但衛生當局與學校的迅速反應旨在確保社區安全。官員們持續密切監控局勢，並承諾在必要時提供最新消息。目前，衛生專家給出的主要建議是保持冷靜、留意症狀，若對個人健康有任何疑慮，請立即尋求醫療協助。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A polite or indirect way of saying that someone has died.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是常見的委婉語，避免直接使用死（die）。文中：The student who passed away attended Henley College in Oxfordshire.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Kept within limits; prevented from spreading.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 被控制在一定範圍內，未擴散。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見意思為「包含」，但在疾病傳播語境下指「被控制住」。文中：Andrew Preston, a professor at the University of Bath, explained that the cases appear to be contained within a small group of people who know each other.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To slowly disappear or become less bright/visible.
keyTerms.noteZh: 通常指顏色或聲音變淡，這裡指皮疹在按壓下不消失。文中：In some cases, the infection can lead to sepsis, which may cause a rash that does not fade when a glass is pressed against it.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Health agency names two schools affected by Berkshire meningitis outbreak

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: One dead and two more ill after meningitis outbreak in Berkshire

quiz.question: Which of the following is true regarding the students affected by the meningitis outbreak?

options.A: All three students attended the same school in Berkshire.
options.B: Two students are currently receiving treatment, while one has died.
options.C: The students who are currently ill attend Henley College.
options.D: The health authorities have confirmed that all affected students were unvaccinated.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段與第二段，文中明確指出有一名就讀 Henley College 的學生不幸過世，另外兩名分別就讀 Reading Blue Coat 與 Highdown 的學生目前正在接受治療。選項 A 錯誤，因為他們就讀不同學校；選項 C 錯誤，因為過世的學生才是在 Henley College；選項 D 文章中並未提及這些學生的疫苗接種狀況。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why are health officials confident that the risk to the general public is low?

options.A: Because the specific strain of meningitis B has been completely eradicated.
options.B: Because the majority of the population in Berkshire has already received the MenB vaccine.
options.C: Because the infection requires close contact to spread and is currently contained within a specific group.
options.D: Because the symptoms of the current strain are too mild to be considered a public health threat.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第三段與第四段，Dr. Mearkle 指出該疾病需要密切接觸才會傳播，且 Professor Preston 強調目前的病例僅侷限於一個互相認識的小群體，並無擴散至社區的跡象。選項 A 文章未提及根除；選項 B 文章未提到 Berkshire 地區的疫苗覆蓋率；選項 D 錯誤，文章明確提到該疾病非常嚴重且可能致命，並非輕微。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'monitor' mean in the context of 'Officials continue to monitor the situation closely'?

options.A: To provide financial support to the affected schools.
options.B: To keep a careful watch over developments to stay informed.
options.C: To force the schools to change their safety policies.
options.D: To publicly announce the names of the infected students.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境中，monitor 的意思是「監控」或「密切觀察」，指衛生官員持續追蹤疫情發展以確保安全。選項 A 與文意不符，官員並非提供金錢支援；選項 C 錯誤，官員是提供指導（guidance）而非強迫改變政策；選項 D 錯誤，文章並未提到要公布學生姓名，這違反隱私原則。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article regarding the meningitis outbreak?

options.A: A new, highly contagious strain of meningitis has emerged in the UK, causing widespread panic.
options.B: Schools in Berkshire are being closed indefinitely to prevent the spread of the disease.
options.C: Health authorities are managing a localized outbreak and advising the public to remain calm and vigilant.
options.D: The current vaccination programs in the UK have failed to protect students from meningitis B.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文主旨為報導 Berkshire 地區的腦膜炎疫情，並強調衛生單位已採取行動控制，同時提醒大眾保持冷靜並注意症狀。選項 A 錯誤，因為該病並不具備高度傳染性；選項 B 錯誤，文中並未提到學校關閉；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到疫苗仍是重要的健康工具，並未指責其失敗。
